name: Guissona and Cervera Loop via Sant Ramon
name_original: De Guissona a Cervera - Circular por Sant Ramon Nonat
description: Cycle 35.3 miles (56.9 km) on this moderate road route from Guissona to Cervera, passing the Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Guissona traverse the open landscapes of the Lleida Plain and the Segarra region. The terrain features a mix of flat sections and rolling hills, characterized by quiet, well-surfaced roads. Cyclists will encounter expansive farmland, fruit orchards, and cereal fields, alongside historical dry stone constructions. The region offers varied environments, from the agricultural plains to areas with more trees and humidity, such as the Vall del Riu Llobregós. Best road cycling routes in Guissona The most popular road cycling route is Guissona and Cervera Loop via Sant Ramon, a 35.3 miles (56.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a total elevation gain of 2262 feet (689.5 meters). Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sanaúj & Biosca - Circular route from Guissona, an easy 20.1 miles (32.4 km) path. This route offers a gentler ride through the local countryside with an elevation gain of 959 feet (292.4 meters). Local road cyclists also love the Solsona – View of Castell Vell d'Olius loop from Guissona, a 54.6 miles (87.9 km) trail leading through the northern Segarra region, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Guissona is defined by open farmland, rolling hills, and views of the Segarra region. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 160 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Guissona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Guissona?
A: The region around Guissona, situated in the Lleida Plain and Segarra, offers a varied landscape for road cyclists. You'll find a mix of flat sections and rolling hills, characterized by quiet, well-surfaced roads. Expect expansive open landscapes, peaceful farmland with fruit orchards and cereal fields, and historical dry stone constructions. Some areas, like the Vall del Riu Llobregós, offer a different environment with more trees and humidity.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Guissona?
A: Yes, Guissona offers routes suitable for various skill levels, including easier options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938571676/sanauj-biosca-circular-route-from-guissona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanaúj & Biosca - Circular route from Guissona&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 20.1-mile (32.4 km) path with a gentler elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed ride through the countryside.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain near Guissona?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking more challenging rides, the region provides routes with significant elevation. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20154289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solsona – View of Castell Vell d'Olius loop from Guissona&lt;/a&gt;, a 54.6-mile (87.9 km) trail that includes substantial climbs, leading through the northern Segarra region.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Guissona?
A: Yes, circular routes are a great way to explore the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938900884/guissona-and-cervera-loop-via-sant-ramon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guissona and Cervera Loop via Sant Ramon&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route covering 35.3 miles (56.9 km). Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938571676/sanauj-biosca-circular-route-from-guissona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanaúj & Biosca - Circular route from Guissona&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easier circular path.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in the Guissona area?
A: Road cycling routes around Guissona often pass by fascinating historical sites. You can encounter numerous dry stone constructions, showcasing historical agricultural practices. For specific landmarks, consider routes that lead near places like the impressive medieval town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montfalcó Murallat&lt;/a&gt; with its preserved 13th-century walls, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features various castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Florejacs Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de l'Aranyó&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views of the Segarra region?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent panoramic views. The region is known for its open landscapes, and routes leading through the northern Segarra, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20154289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solsona – View of Castell Vell d'Olius loop from Guissona&lt;/a&gt;, often offer scenic vistas of the area and the town of Guissona itself from various vantage points.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Guissona?
A: The region around Guissona is generally appealing for road cycling due to its diverse landscapes. While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the open landscapes and varied terrain suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling, avoiding the potential heat of summer or colder conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any specific natural features or unique environments to explore by bike?
A: Beyond the expansive farmland, you can explore unique natural environments. The Vall del Riu Llobregós, just 9 km north of Guissona, offers a different setting with more trees and increased humidity, including interesting spots like the Molí del Cava with its waterfall. Cyclists will also encounter numerous dry stone constructions throughout the paths, which are a historical feature of the landscape.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes that pass by castles near Guissona?
A: Yes, the Guissona area is rich in historical castles. Routes can take you past impressive structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Florejacs Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de l'Aranyó&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Concabella Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20153164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Pallargues Castle – Guissona Old Town loop from Guissona&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that specifically includes a castle.

Q: What is the overall difficulty range of road cycling routes available in Guissona?
A: Guissona offers a good range of road cycling routes catering to different abilities. You can find easy routes for leisurely rides, moderate routes that provide a good workout, and more difficult options for experienced cyclists seeking significant climbs and longer distances. The region's mix of flat plains and rolling hills contributes to this diversity.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes starting from Guissona?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, Guissona serves as a starting point for extended routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castell Vell d'Olius – Solsona loop from Guissona&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route spanning nearly 60 miles (96.5 km), offering a substantial distance for endurance cyclists.

Municipality and head of the region of La Segarra, in the center of the riverside of Cervera, extended on both sides of the river of Cervera or river of Ondara, on top of the structural platforms of La Segarra. The town can be seen from afar as it is located on top of a hill.

Ramon, son of Portell, died in the castle of Cardona. In the chapel, with a salt crystal on the facade, there is the phrase carved: "Aci es nat Sant Ramon Nonat". There is also a reliquary, where the only preserved remains of the saint are venerated.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route connecting Guissona and Cervera, you can typically find parking in either town to begin your ride. Both are established settlements with public parking options.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' for racebikes, covering approximately 56.9 kilometers (35.4 miles) with an elevation gain of around 690 meters (2,264 feet). While mostly on well-paved roads, it requires a good level of fitness and is not typically recommended for absolute beginners due to its length and elevation.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily a road cycling trail, meaning you can expect mostly well-paved surfaces suitable for racebikes. However, as a 'moderate' route in this region, there might be short segments that are unpaved or present more challenging conditions.

faqs.question: What interesting places can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route connects the towns of Guissona and Cervera. A significant highlight is the Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat, which is a key point of interest mentioned in the trail's name and offers cultural significance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the region, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite hot, while winter might bring colder weather, though road cycling is often possible year-round depending on specific conditions.

faqs.question: Is this a circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as indicated by its name, 'From Guissona to Cervera - Circular via Sant Ramon Nonat,' this is a circular route, meaning you will end your ride in the same location where you started.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other trails, including 'El Camí IT2TR3 El Camí de Ponent: de Balaguer a Montserrat', 'Ruta del Monestir. La Segarra', and 'Vall del Llobregós'.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route in the Lleida region.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike route primarily on roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, cycling with a dog on a leash alongside a racebike on public roads is not recommended for safety reasons for both the cyclist and the dog.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects the towns of Guissona and Cervera, you will find various cafes, restaurants, and shops in both settlements where you can stop for food and drinks.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given it's a moderate racebike route of nearly 57 km, you should bring appropriate cycling gear, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and sun protection. A helmet is essential, and consider carrying a phone for navigation and emergencies.
